steps: In your CRM, create a new API key that is read-only — no edit, no delete, no export-all — and confirm in the CRM's own settings screen that it physically cannot write.
Save that key in a file called .env in your project folder, and never paste it anywhere else — not in CLAUDE.md, not in an email, not in Slack.
Open Claude Code in that folder and ask it to add a deny rule in settings.json blocking any write or delete command against the CRM.
Ask it to explain in plain English what that deny rule actually blocks, so you know it did what you asked before you rely on it.
Ask Claude for the donor segment summary you need — aggregate counts and totals only, never a request for named records.
Read the result back and confirm every line is a count or a total, with no donor name, email, or address anywhere in it.
verify: Go back to your CRM's key management screen and confirm the key shows read-only. If it shows any write or delete permission, delete the key, generate a new one, and redo the setup before using it again.
